title: Implement end-to-end encryption for sending messages

requirements.text: Ensure outgoing messages are end-to-end encrypted on the client before calling the backend sendMessage API: generate the ISO 20022 / SWIFT raw plaintext locally, encrypt it using a per-message AES-GCM key, encrypt that AES key with the recipient’s registered RSA public key (RSA-OAEP), and only send ciphertext + encrypted AES key to the backend (never plaintext).

quotes: implement end-to-end encryption for send messages

acceptanceCriteria: When sending a message from ComposeMessagePage, the backend sendMessage call is made with encryptedPayload and keyId/encrypted key bytes only (no plaintext is sent).
If the recipient does not have a publicKey registered, the UI blocks sending and shows a clear error in English.
Encryption uses WebCrypto (AES-GCM + RSA-OAEP) and produces different ciphertext for the same plaintext on repeated sends (fresh IV / key per message).

requirements.text: Make message retrieval consistent with end-to-end encrypted messaging by returning both received and sent messages for the authenticated user (so the inbox can show both directions), while keeping message content stored and transported only as encrypted bytes.

quotes: implement end-to-end encryption for send messages

acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo getMessages returns messages where (m.to == caller) OR (m.from == caller).
The returned Message records still contain only encryptedPayload + keyId (encrypted symmetric key) and do not include any plaintext fields.
Frontend InboxPage shows both 'Received' and 'Sent' badges for the current user after this change (without requiring frontend workarounds like calling admin APIs).

requirements.text: Ensure recipients can decrypt message content client-side using only their locally stored private key (device-only key), and display a clear error when decryption fails (e.g., message not intended for this recipient or missing local private key).

quotes: implement end-to-end encryption for send messages

acceptanceCriteria: Opening a received message in MessageDetailPage decrypts successfully when the recipient has their transport private key available locally.
If the user lacks a local keyPair/privateKey, MessageDetailPage shows an English error state indicating the device cannot decrypt the message.
If a non-recipient attempts to decrypt a message (or ciphertext/key mismatch occurs), the UI shows an English error and does not display any plaintext.

requirements.text: Clarify naming and intent of the encrypted symmetric key field end-to-end (frontend + backend types), so it is not mislabeled as a vetKey: keep compatibility where possible, but ensure code and type names/documentation clearly indicate this field is the RSA-OAEP encrypted per-message symmetric key used for E2EE decryption.

quotes: implement end-to-end encryption for send messages

acceptanceCriteria: Code identifiers and inline comments no longer imply that the stored per-message encrypted key is a “vetKey” (it is the encrypted symmetric key for E2EE).
Frontend and backend still interoperate after the rename/clarification (send -> store -> list -> decrypt flow works).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional backend services).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only UI component sources); compose them instead.
Do not modify immutable hook files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx).
No third-party auth providers; Internet Identity only.
No external databases or non-Motoko backend code.
nonGoals: Adding third-party cryptography services or server-side decryption.
Adding new message types beyond ISO 20022 and SWIFT.
